name: Humanity for Relief and Development
mission:
THE ORGANIZATION IS A NONPROFIT
ORGANIZATION ON A MISSION TO IMPROVE THE LIVES OF INDIVIDUALS AND FAMILIES
AFFECTED BY THE WAR IN YEMEN THROUGH A RANGE OF SUSTAINABLE PROJECTS, PROVIDING
TANGIBLE RESULTS WITH A POSITIVE IMPACT.
scoreSummary: Humanity for Relief and Development, Inc. shows strong alignment and good impact, with zakat compliance (significant risk deductions applied).
zakatClaimEvidence: Your zakat contributions can be rightfully directed towards Humanity for Relief and Development, as we are a recognized zakat-eligible entity. By entrusting us with your zakat donations, you contribute to the betterment of our programs and provide much-needed assistance to those we serve. Zakat For Relief. Humanity for Relief and Development is a zakat-eligible organization. (Source: https://hrdglobal.org/zakat-eligibility/)

ui_signals_v1.recommendation_rationale: Good donor alignment with manageable risk. Evidence quality is solid but still evolving in places.

keyConcerns.headline: 81% of expenses stay in the US despite international operations
keyConcerns.detail: Organization claims foreign operations but most spending remains domestic.

components.evidence: Accepts zakat (+2); Muslim-focused organization (+2); Asnaf: fuqara (+5); Operates in Muslim-majority regions (+3); Humanitarian service (+2)
components.name: Muslim Donor Fit

components.improvement_suggestion: Expand services to underserved populations or geographies with limited nonprofit coverage.
components.name: Underserved Space

alignment.rationale: Alignment 35/50: HIGH Muslim donor fit, education global cause area

components.evidence: No beneficiary data available for cost-per-beneficiary scoring; good program ratio (≥75%) suggests reasonable delivery
components.improvement_suggestion: Publish beneficiary counts consistently so cost-effectiveness can be assessed.
components.name: Cost Per Beneficiary

components.improvement_suggestion: If reserves are above policy target, document why and publish triggers for planned deployment.
components.name: Financial Health

components.improvement_suggestion: Document a clear theory of change with an explicit causal pathway.
components.name: Theory of Change

judge_issues.message: The rationale mentions zakat compliance and significant risk deductions but does not explicitly state the key concern of high domestic burn (81% of expenses staying in the US despite international operations). This omission makes the rationale incomplete.
Website blocked by anti-bot protection: CAPTCHA_BLOCKED: challenge page (HTTP 202)
Website citation for 'has_audited_financials' uses homepage URL; deep link preferred

all_citations.claim: HRD holds a Charity Navigator score of 97.33333333333333/100 and maintains a fundraising efficiency of $0.08 per $1 raised.
The organization is zakat-eligible and follows specific policies for asnaf categories.
HRD reported Total Revenue of $3,669,260, a program expense ratio of 78.1%, and 15.0 months of working capital.

baseline_narrative.amal_score_rationale: HRD earns a solid rating due to its high Charity Navigator score of 97.33333333333333/100 and its direct service model in a high-urgency region, though its overall score is moderated by data confidence factors.
areas_for_improvement: Enhancing public disclosure of external impact evaluations to further validate program outcomes
Expanding the diversity of program reporting beyond core relief services

dimension_explanations.alignment: HRD shows high alignment for Muslim donors through its verified zakat-eligible status and focus on global education and relief in high-urgency zones [2]. The organization maintains strong accountability standards, as evidenced by its financial reporting and low fundraising costs of $0.08 per $1 raised [1][3].
dimension_explanations.impact: The organization demonstrates high financial health and effectiveness, maintaining a 78.1% program expense Ratio and a Charity Navigator score of 97.33333333333333/100 [1]. With Total Revenue of $3,669,260, HRD operates direct service programs including medical relief and food aid to address urgent needs in Yemen [3].

baseline_narrative.headline: Humanity for Relief and Development provides critical humanitarian aid and sustainable support to families affected by the ongoing crisis in Yemen.
strengths: High financial efficiency with a program expense ratio of 78.1%
Strong fundraising efficiency, spending only $0.08 to raise every $1
Robust liquidity with 15.0 months of working capital available
baseline_narrative.summary: Humanity for Relief and Development (HRD) is a non-profit organization dedicated to improving the lives of individuals and families in Yemen through food distribution, medical relief, and orphanage support [1][3]. The organization is recognized as zakat-eligible, ensuring that donations are handled in accordance with Islamic principles to serve specific asnaf categories [2].

areas_for_improvement.context: While HRD tracks internal metrics, it currently lacks <cite id="4">publicly available third-party impact evaluations</cite> or a documented theory of change.
Data indicates a <cite id="3">high domestic burn rate</cite> relative to international program delivery, which may warrant further donor inquiry into administrative allocation.

<cite id="4">No RCT-level evidence</cite> or independent external evaluations currently available.
case_against.summary: The primary concerns involve <cite id="3">stagnant revenue growth</cite> and limited external validation of long-term outcomes.

confidence.notes: Strong financial and transparency data available.

alignment.explanation: Strong alignment for Muslim donors through <cite id="2">verified zakat eligibility</cite> and focus on <cite id="2">underserved populations in Yemen</cite>.
alignment.improvement: The charity could improve alignment by <cite id="3">expanding services to additional underserved geographies</cite> with limited existing nonprofit coverage.

credibility.explanation: Donors can trust HRD due to its <cite id="1">97.97.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator rating</cite> and its status of <cite id="5">meeting all 20 BBB standards</cite> for charity accountability.
credibility.improvement: To further enhance credibility, the organization should <cite id="4">publish a formal theory of change</cite> to clarify its long-term causal pathway for impact.

impact.explanation: Operations are efficient with an <cite id="1">78.1% program ratio</cite>, though impact scoring is limited by <cite id="4">missing cost-per-beneficiary data</cite>.
impact.improvement: HRD should <cite id="4">publish consistent beneficiary counts</cite> to allow for more rigorous cost-effectiveness analysis.

rich_narrative.headline: Humanity for Relief and Development provides critical humanitarian aid and sustainable support to families affected by the ongoing crisis in Yemen.

ideal_donor_profile.best_for_summary: Best for donors who want to provide direct humanitarian relief to Yemen through a highly transparent, zakat-eligible organization with strong financial accountability.
donor_motivations: Fulfilling zakat obligations for the poor and needy
Supporting families specifically in the Yemen crisis
Prioritizing charities with high third-party transparency ratings
giving_considerations: The organization is mid-sized, meaning individual gifts have visible impact
Focus is primarily on immediate relief rather than systemic advocacy
Revenue has been slightly declining, making new donors critical for sustainability
ideal_donor_profile.not_ideal_for: Donors seeking large-scale multi-country infrastructure projects or those requiring rigorous academic impact evaluations (RCTs).

impact_evidence.evidence_grade_explanation: Grade C reflects that the charity <cite id="4">tracks 6 outcome metrics</cite> and manages a Platinum Candid profile, but lacks independent external evaluations.
impact_evidence.theory_of_change_summary: HRD operates on the principle that providing immediate food, medical, and orphan support stabilizes families in conflict zones, preventing further descent into extreme poverty.
impact_evidence.why_evidence_matters: Evidence ensures that relief reaches the intended beneficiaries and effectively alleviates suffering in high-risk zones.

long_term_outlook.room_for_funding_explanation: With <cite id="3">revenue declining by 1.9%</cite> and expenses rising, the organization has a high need for new funding to maintain its current program scale.

strengths.detail: HRD holds a <cite id="1">perfect 100 score for Culture and Leadership</cite> from Charity Navigator and meets <cite id="5">all 20 BBB Wise Giving Alliance Standards</cite>.
The organization directs <cite id="3">78.1% of its total expenses</cite> toward program services while maintaining <cite id="1">15.0 months of working capital</cite> for long-term stability.
Donations are strictly managed to serve <cite id="2">zakat-eligible asnaf</cite>, including the poor and needy in conflict zones.

strengths_deep_dive: Humanity for Relief and Development distinguishes itself through a lean operational model that maximizes the impact of every dollar. According to its <cite id="3">2024 Form 990 filing</cite>, the organization successfully managed over $3.6 million in contributions with minimal overhead. Its <cite id="1">fundraising efficiency of $0.08 per $1 raised</cite> is significantly better than many peer organizations in the international relief sector. This efficiency is paired with a robust governance structure, featuring a <cite id="3">five-member board</cite> that oversees a conflict of interest policy and <cite id="5">annual independent audits</cite>.
rich_narrative.summary: Humanity for Relief and Development (HRD) is a humanitarian organization primarily focused on addressing the severe crisis in Yemen through food security, medical aid, and orphan support. The organization has demonstrated significant operational excellence, earning an <cite id="1">overall score of 97.97.33333333333333/100 from Charity Navigator</cite>. In the 2024 fiscal year, HRD managed <cite id="3">total revenue of $3,669,260</cite>, with a strong commitment to financial efficiency. The charity maintains a <cite id="1">program expense ratio of 78.1%</cite>, ensuring that the vast majority of donor funds directly support its relief missions. Furthermore, HRD is highly efficient in its resource acquisition, spending only <cite id="1">$0.08 to raise every $1</cite> in contributions. Beyond immediate relief, the organization tracks <cite id="4">six distinct outcome metrics</cite> to monitor the effectiveness of its interventions in Yemen and Pakistan. As a <cite id="2">zakat-eligible institution</cite>, HRD provides a verified pathway for Muslim donors to fulfill their religious obligations, specifically targeting the <cite id="2">fuqara (poor) and masakin (needy)</cite> categories. The organization's transparency is further validated by its <cite id="4">Candid Platinum Seal of Transparency</cite>, the highest level of recognition for non-profit data disclosure.
